title: RPYR-0014 — the hash dispatch is unguarded: `PyObject::hash` (protocol/object.rs:695) has no `with_recursion` while repr/compare do, so `genericalias`/`slice`/`code` (and tuple, RPYR-0013) `__hash__` overflow the native stack → SIGSEGV
one_line_repro:
x=int
for _ in range(400000): x=list[x]
hash(x)   # SIGSEGV; slice(slice(...)) hash too
prior_art: RustPython umbrella #2796 (open, "Recursions in rust code trigger segmentation faults") = fuzzer RUSTPY-0007a (which names hash/compare). RPYR-0013 (tuple.__hash__) is the same class. Shared with CPython (genericalias/slice hash route through tuple_hash there) = CPython #154318.
found_by: recursion-guard-auditor (informed whole-tree explore — found the SYSTEMIC root: hash dispatch at object.rs:695 lacks the guard that repr(:383)/compare(:291) have)
git-history-analyzer (independently flagged genericalias.__hash__ as an RPYR-0013 sibling)
toolkit_class: unguarded-protocol-recursion (native stack overflow, NOT a catchable RecursionError)
cpython_behavior: shares the crash for genericalias/slice hash (both route through CPython tuple_hash, filed as #154318). RustPython's fix is systemic and one-line.
guarded_twin: SAME file, SAME dispatch layer: `PyObject::repr` (protocol/object.rs:383) and `PyObject::_cmp`/rich-compare (:291) both wrap their slot dispatch in `vm.with_recursion(...)` — verified live, deep repr and deep `<`/`==` raise a clean RecursionError. Only `PyObject::hash` (:695) omits it. One-line fix there guards EVERY `__hash__` at once (tuple RPYR-0013 included).
affected: any hash of these as a dict key / set member (insertion hashes)
notes: The recursion-guard agent traced the crash past the per-slot view (RPYR-0013 fixed only tuple.rs) to the DISPATCH: `PyObject::hash` (protocol/object.rs:695) calls the type's hash slot with no `vm.with_recursion` wrap, so any element-wise __hash__ recurses one native frame per level → stack overflow → SIGSEGV. Verified live: `x=int; {x=list[x]}x4e5; hash(x)` SIGSEGVs (genericalias), `slice(slice(...))x2e6; hash()` SIGSEGVs (slice) — both also SIGSEGV on CPython (shared, #154318). FIX: mirror :291/:383 — wrap the hash dispatch at object.rs:695 in `vm.with_recursion("while hashing", ...)`; this subsumes RPYR-0013's per-slot tuple.rs fix and covers genericalias/slice/code too. Note: frozenset/union/range hash are NOT affected (cached bottom-up at construction / bounded). Toolkit calibration: the scanner missed all of these because the recursion is dispatched through free fns / the slot table, not visible in the slot body — the SYSTEMIC signal is the dispatch site.
